english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus autem manifeste dicit, quia in novissimis temporibus discedent quidam a fide, attendentes spiritibus erroris, et doctrinis dæmoniorum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ πνεῦμα ῥητῶς λέγει ὅτι ἐν ὑστέροις καιροῖς ἀποστήσονταί τινες τῆς πίστεως προσέχοντες πνεύμασιν πλάνοις καὶ διδασκαλίαις δαιμονίων
verses.king_james: Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;

verses.latin_vulgate: in hypocrisi loquentium mendacium, et cauteriatam habentium suam conscientiam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ὑποκρίσει ψευδολόγων κεκαυτηριασμένων τὴν ἰδίαν συνείδησιν
verses.king_james: Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;

verses.latin_vulgate: prohibentium nubere, abstinere a cibis, quod Deus creavit ad percipiendum cum gratiarum actione fidelibus, et iis qui cognoverunt veritatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κωλυόντων γαμεῖν ἀπέχεσθαι βρωμάτων ἃ ὁ θεὸς ἔκτισεν εἰς μετάληψιν μετὰ εὐχαριστίας τοῖς πιστοῖς καὶ ἐπεγνωκόσιν τὴν ἀλήθειαν
verses.king_james: Forbidding to marry, *and commanding* to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia omnis creatura Dei bona est, et nihil rejiciendum quod cum gratiarum actione percipitur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι πᾶν κτίσμα θεοῦ καλόν καὶ οὐδὲν ἀπόβλητον μετὰ εὐχαριστίας λαμβανόμενον
verses.king_james: For every creature of God *is* good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:

verses.latin_vulgate: sanctificatur enim per verbum Dei, et orationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἁγιάζεται γὰρ διὰ λόγου θεοῦ καὶ ἐντεύξεως
verses.king_james: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc proponens fratribus, bonus eris minister Christi Jesu enutritus verbis fidei, et bonæ doctrinæ, quam assecutus es.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα ὑποτιθέμενος τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς καλὸς ἔσῃ διάκονος ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ ἐντρεφόμενος τοῖς λόγοις τῆς πίστεως καὶ τῆς καλῆς διδασκαλίας ᾗ παρηκολούθηκας
verses.king_james: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ineptas autem, et aniles fabulas devita: exerce autem teipsum ad pietatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοὺς δὲ βεβήλους καὶ γραώδεις μύθους παραιτοῦ γύμναζε δὲ σεαυτὸν πρὸς εὐσέβειαν
verses.king_james: But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself *rather* unto godliness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam corporalis exercitatio, ad modicum utilis est: pietas autem ad omnia utilis est, promissionem habens vitæ, quæ nunc est, et futuræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ σωματικὴ γυμνασία πρὸς ὀλίγον ἐστὶν ὠφέλιμος ἡ δὲ εὐσέβεια πρὸς πάντα ὠφέλιμός ἐστιν ἐπαγγελίαν ἔχουσα ζωῆς τῆς νῦν καὶ τῆς μελλούσης
verses.king_james: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fidelis sermo, et omni acceptione dignus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστὸς ὁ λόγος καὶ πάσης ἀποδοχῆς ἄξιος
verses.king_james: This *is* a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc enim laboramus, et maledicimur, quia speramus in Deum vivum, qui est Salvator omnium hominum, maxime fidelium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ καὶ κοπιῶμεν καὶ ὀνειδιζόμεθα ὅτι ἠλπίκαμεν ἐπὶ θεῷ ζῶντι ὅς ἐστιν σωτὴρ πάντων ἀνθρώπων μάλιστα πιστῶν
verses.king_james: For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo adolescentiam tuam contemnat: sed exemplum esto fidelium in verbo, in conversatione, in caritate, in fide, in castitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδείς σου τῆς νεότητος καταφρονείτω ἀλλὰ τύπος γίνου τῶν πιστῶν ἐν λόγῳ ἐν ἀναστροφῇ ἐν ἀγάπῃ ἐν πνεύματι ἐν πίστει ἐν ἁγνείᾳ
verses.king_james: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum venio, attende lectioni, exhortationi, et doctrinæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕως ἔρχομαι πρόσεχε τῇ ἀναγνώσει τῇ παρακλήσει τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ
verses.king_james: Till I come, give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli negligere gratiam, quæ in te est, quæ data est tibi per prophetiam, cum impositione manuum presbyterii.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ἀμέλει τοῦ ἐν σοὶ χαρίσματος ὃ ἐδόθη σοι διὰ προφητείας μετὰ ἐπιθέσεως τῶν χειρῶν τοῦ πρεσβυτερίου
verses.king_james: Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc meditare, in his esto: ut profectus tuus manifestus sit omnibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα μελέτα ἐν τούτοις ἴσθι ἵνα σου ἡ προκοπὴ φανερὰ ᾖ ἐν πᾶσιν
verses.king_james: Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attende tibi, et doctrinæ: insta in illis. Hoc enim faciens, et teipsum salvum facies, et eos qui te audiunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔπεχε σεαυτῷ καὶ τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ ἐπίμενε αὐτοῖς τοῦτο γὰρ ποιῶν καὶ σεαυτὸν σώσεις καὶ τοὺς ἀκούοντάς σου
verses.king_james: Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself, and them that hear thee.
